target.name: Databricks
target.founder: Ali Ghodsi, Ion Stoica, Matei Zaharia, Patrick Wendell, Reynold Xin, Andy Konwinski, Arsalan Tavakoli-Shiraji

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $29.3B - $400.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Databricks, a Series L AI & Software company, is valued at $134B against a comparable universe of 8 same-sector peers spanning both public and private stages. The peer set includes Palantir (public, $400B), OpenAI ($300B), xAI ($250B), Anthropic ($183B), and Waymo ($126B), reflecting the breadth of valuations within frontier AI infrastructure.

Peer-median valuation analysis—the primary triangulation method applicable given available data—yields a median comparable valuation of $154.5B across the 8-peer cohort. The peer range spans $29.3B to $400.0B, reflecting wide dispersion in stage and model maturity within the AI software sector. This peer-median approach directly anchors the valuation framework without adjustment for company-specific revenue multiples, margin profiles, or growth rates, which are not provided in the data set.

The triangulated valuation range supports $131.3B (low), $154.5B (median), and $177.7B (high), with the current $134B valuation sitting modestly below peer median. Key uncertainties include the single methodology applied (peer-median only), absence of revenue and profitability data to contextualize peer comparables, and the heterogeneity of stages and business models within the comp set itself. Further refinement would require margin analysis, growth-rate normalization, and customer concentration metrics.
